blocks.content: The week moved the way bad weeks moved — slowly in the moment, and then all at once gone.

Daisy came to school. She sat in homeroom and said hey and answered questions in class and ate lunch with Savannah. She wasn't cold to him — it was somehow worse than cold. She was just slightly less available, like a radio station going just a little off frequency. Still there. Just with static now.
Kit tried twice more to catch her alone at her locker. Both times she had somewhere to be.
Eli watched all of this and said nothing, which was the most alarming thing Eli had ever done.
On Thursday Savannah stopped at Kit's locker in a way that was not accidental.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: She's not going to bring it up. You know that, right.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: She's going to let you off the hook if you let it go. And you're going to let it go because it's easier.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I'm not trying to be hard on you. I just — you know her. She'll bury it and act fine until she actually is fine and you'll never know the difference. Is that what you want?
She walked away. Kit stood at his locker for a long moment. Then he closed it and went to C-wing.
